summary: Justin Brennan walks through live analysis of a 43-unit Fort Worth multifamily property listed at $5M (seller asking $4M), teaching how to identify distressed seller motivation through loan analysis, verify suspicious financials, and underwrite deals to actual market value rather than asking price. Key insight: the seller bought in Feb 2023 for $2.6M with a likely 3-year note coming due in Feb 2026, creating forced-sale pressure that justifies aggressive underwriting.
takeaways: Commercial loans are 3, 5, 7, or 10-year terms (not 30-year like residential) - amortization schedule is separate from loan maturity date
Always ask sellers: 'Is your loan assumable?' and 'What's your current debt balance?' to find loan records in county data or Real Estate Radar software
Request last 12 months of bank statements to verify T12/P&L - owner-provided financials are unreliable; use bank statements for 'trust but verify'
Don't send detailed due diligence questions until LOI is signed/inked - brokers won't respond to questions before contract phase
Apply minimum 45% expense ratio to any owner-provided revenue numbers for properties of this age/type to back-calculate actual NOI
Expect Section 8 cap at max 10% of units per your portfolio - banks will ask this question and high percentages create operational/legal risk

For distressed sellers showing equity loss: they face three options: (1) refinance with bank if willing, (2) sell at loss to avoid foreclosure, or (3) hand keys back to bank - use this framework in negotiations
Track new lease spikes (question: how many new leases in last 90 days?) - large recent spike indicates owner filling vacancy before sale, shifting tenant risk to buyer

skill_candidates.change: Systematically verify seller motivation through loan term analysis before underwriting offer price
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin demonstrates pulling property debt ($1.945M balance, 6.33% rate, likely 3-year term from Feb 2023 = Feb 2026 maturity) from Real Estate Radar to identify forced-sale window and pressure; frames this as 'you can smell it' - the key motivator for negotiations

skill_candidates.change: Always request 12 months of bank statements as verification step after receiving seller's P&L/T12
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin explicitly states 'bank statements, got to do that' and predicts seller will 'pucker up' when asked, proving financials are fudged; demonstrates calling the bluff by requesting bank statements specifically to validate revenue claims

skill_candidates.change: Apply conservative 45% minimum expense ratio assumption when underwriting owner-provided numbers for older multifamily
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin states 'I guarantee this building is not operating for less than a 45% expense ratio, period' for this 1901-built property; tells Luis to 'take whatever revenue he's showing and minimum 45% expense ratio' to back into real NOI

skill_candidates.change: Recognize when property is off-market due to broker rejection, using Zillow self-listing as red flag for overpricing
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin explains 'no brokers would take his listing' and 'no commercial broker' because seller's $5M ask exceeds broker opinion of value (~$3M); self-listing on Zillow indicates seller 'doesn't know what he's doing' and is underwater on 2023 purchase

title: Tuesday Mentorship - Distressed Deal Dive_06-24-25 | Justin Brennan
